key_points: Health includes physical, mental, and social well-being
A balanced lifestyle includes nutritious food, regular exercise, adequate sleep, and stress management
Diseases may be communicable or non-communicable
Communicable diseases spread through pathogens via air, water, food, contact, or vectors
Non-communicable diseases are often caused by lifestyle or genetic factors
Vaccination helps the body develop immunity against specific diseases
Antibiotics are used to treat bacterial infections
examples: Vector-borne disease: Malaria spreads through Anopheles mosquito
Airborne disease: Tuberculosis spreads through air droplets
Waterborne disease: Cholera spreads through contaminated water
Lifestyle disease: Diabetes caused by poor diet and lack of exercise
Vaccination example: Polio vaccine protects children from polio infection
formulas_or_concepts: Health = Physical well-being + Mental well-being + Social well-being
Disease transmission = Pathogen + Host + Mode of transmission
Immunity develops when the body recognizes and fights pathogens
Vaccines introduce weakened or inactive pathogens to stimulate immunity
prevention_measures: Ensure proper sanitation and waste disposal
Exercise regularly and maintain a balanced diet
exam_tips: Understand the WHO definition of health
Know examples of communicable and non-communicable diseases
Remember different modes of disease transmission
Understand the role of vaccines and antibiotics
